name: InNat

values.name: add
values.comment:
Add a `Nat (In ...)`.

    NNat.toIn nat3
        |> InNat.add between1And12 nat1 nat12
    --> of type Nat (In Nat4 (Nat15Plus a))
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In addedMin addedMax addedMaybeMax) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N addedMin (Nat.Bound.Is min Nat.Bound.To) sumMin x) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N addedMax (Nat.Bound.Is max Nat.Bound.To) sumMax y) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn sumMin sumMax)

values.name: addLossy
values.comment:
Add a `Nat (In ...)`, but

  - keep the current minimum, instead of computing the exact value

  - use `Infinity` as the maximum instead of computing the exact value

    atLeast5 |> MinNat.addLossy atLeast2
    --> is of type Nat (ValueMin Nat5)

    atLeast2 |> MinNat.addLossy atLeast5
    --> is of type Nat (ValueMin Nat2)

If you know the minimum and maximum added value, use [`add`](InNat#add).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In addedMin addedMax addedMaybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueMin min)

values.name: addN
values.comment:
Add a fixed `Nat (N ...)` value.

    nat70 |> NNat.toIn
        |> InNat.addN nat7
    --> is of type Nat (In Nat77 (Nat77Plus a))
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N added (Nat.Bound.Is min Nat.Bound.To) sumMin (Nat.Bound.And max Nat.Bound.To sumMax)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn sumMin sumMax)

values.name: atMost
values.comment:
**Cap** the `Nat (In ...)` to at most a number.

    nat5 |> NNat.toIn
        |> InNat.atMost nat10 { min = nat5 }
    --> InNat 5

    nat15 |> NNat.toIn |> InNat.lowerMin nat5
        |> InNat.atMost nat10 { min = nat5 }
    --> Nat 10

`min` ensures that that number is at least the minimum.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In minNewMax newMaxPlusA newMaxMaybeN) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min (Nat.Bound.Is minToMinNewMax Nat.Bound.To) minNewMax x) } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn min newMaxPlusA)

values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N tried (Nat.Bound.Is triedToMax Nat.Bound.To) max (Nat.Bound.And a Nat.Bound.To (TypeNats.Nat1Plus triedPlusAMinus1))) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min (Nat.Bound.Is minToTried Nat.Bound.To) tried y) } -> { equal : () -> result, less : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn min triedPlusAMinus1) -> result, greater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn (TypeNats.Nat2Plus triedMinus1) max) -> result } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> result

values.name: isAtLeast
values.comment:
Is the `Nat (In ...)`

  - `equalOrGreater` than a `Nat` or

  - `less`?

```
vote : { age : Nat (In (Nat18Plus orOlder) max) } -> Vote

tryToVote =
    InNat.lowerMin nat0
        >> InNat.isAtLeast nat18
            { min = nat0 }
            { less = Nothing --😓
            , equalOrGreater = \age -> Just (vote { age = age })
            }
```
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N tried (Nat.Bound.Is a Nat.Bound.To) (TypeNats.Nat1Plus triedMinus1PlusA) (Nat.Bound.And atLeastRange Nat.Bound.To max)) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min (Nat.Bound.Is a Nat.Bound.To) minPlusA (Nat.Bound.And (TypeNats.Nat1Plus lessRange) Nat.Bound.To tried)) } -> { less : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn min triedMinus1PlusA) -> result, equalOrGreater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn tried max) -> result } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> result

values.name: isAtMost
values.comment:
Is the `Nat (In ...)`

  - `equalOrLess` than a `Nat` or

  - `greater`?

```
goToU18Party : { age : Nat (In min Nat17) } -> List Snack

tryToGoToU18Party =
    InNat.lowerMin nat0
        >> InNat.isAtMost nat17
            { min = nat0 }
            { equalOrLess = \age -> Just (goToU18Party { age 0 age })
            , greater = Nothing
            }
```
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N tried (Nat.Bound.Is a Nat.Bound.To) triedPlusA (Nat.Bound.And (TypeNats.Nat1Plus greaterRange) Nat.Bound.To max)) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min (Nat.Bound.Is minToTried Nat.Bound.To) tried y) } -> { equalOrLess : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn min triedPlusA) -> result, greater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn tried max) -> result } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> result

values.name: isInRange
values.comment:
Compared to a range `first` to `last`, is the `Nat (In ...)`

  - `inRange`

  - `greater` than the `last` or

  - `less` than the `first`?

```
justIfBetween3And10 =
    InNat.lowerMin nat0
        >> InNat.isInRange { first = nat3, last = nat10 }
            { min = nat0 }
            { less = \_ -> Nothing
            , greater = \_ -> Nothing
            , inRange = Just
            }

justIfBetween3And10 nat9
--> Just (Nat 9)

justIfBetween3And10 nat123
--> Nothing
```
values.type: { first : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N first (Nat.Bound.Is firstToLast Nat.Bound.To) last (Nat.Bound.And a Nat.Bound.To (TypeNats.Nat1Plus firstMinus1PlusA))), last : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N last (Nat.Bound.Is lastToMax Nat.Bound.To) max (Nat.Bound.And a Nat.Bound.To lastPlusA)) } -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min (Nat.Bound.Is minToFirst Nat.Bound.To) first x) } -> { inRange : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn first lastPlusA) -> result, less : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn min firstMinus1PlusA) -> result, greater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn (TypeNats.Nat1Plus last) max) -> result } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> result

values.name: maxIs
values.comment:
Make it fit into functions with require a higher maximum.

You should design type annotations as general as possible.

    onlyAtMost18 : Nat (In min Nat18 maybeN)

    onlyAtMost18 between3And8 --fine

But once you implement `onlyAtMost18`, you might use the value in `onlyAtMost19`.

    onlyAtMost18 value =
        -- onlyAtMost19 value --error :(
        onlyAtMost19
            (value |> InNat.maxIs nat18 {- works :) -})

[`lowerMin`](InNat#lowerMin) is also handy in those situations.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N max (Nat.Bound.Is a Nat.Bound.To) maxPlusA x) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn min maxPlusA)

values.name: sub
values.comment:
Subtract a `Nat (In ...)`.

The 2 following arguments are

  - the minimum subtracted value
  - the maximum subtracted value

If you don't have both at hand, use [`subLossy`](InNat#subLossy).

    nat6 |> NNat.toIn
        |> InNat.sub between1And5 nat1 nat5
    --> is of type Nat (In Nat1 (Nat5Plus a))
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In minSubbed maxSubbed subbedMaybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N minSubbed (Nat.Bound.Is differenceMax Nat.Bound.To) max x) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N maxSubbed (Nat.Bound.Is differenceMin Nat.Bound.To) min y) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn differenceMin differenceMax)

values.name: subLossy
values.comment:
Subtract a `Nat (In ..)` without

  - calculating the new minimum, the lowest possible value it can be after the subtraction is 0

```
nat6 |> NNat.toMin
    |> InNat.subLossy between1And5
--> is of type Nat (In Nat0 Infinity)

nat6 |> NNat.toIn
    |> InNat.subLossy between1And5
--> is of type Nat (In Nat0 (Nat6Plus a))
```

If you know the maximum subtracted value, use [`subLossyMax`](InNat#subLossyMax).

If you also know the minimum subtracted value, use [`sub`](InNat#sub).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In minSubbed min subbedMaybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn TypeNats.Nat0 max)

values.name: subN
values.comment:
Subtract a fixed `Nat` value.

    nat7 |> NNat.toIn
        |> InNat.subN nat7
    --> is of type Nat (In Nat0 a)

**Use [`MinNat.subN`](MinNat#subN) if the maximum value is `Infinity`**.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N sub (Nat.Bound.Is differenceMin Nat.Bound.To) min (Nat.Bound.And differenceMax Nat.Bound.To max)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn differenceMin differenceMax)

values.name: toMin
values.comment:
Convert an exact `Nat (In min ...)` to a `Nat (ValueMin min)`.

    in4To10 |> NNat.toMin
    --> is of type Nat (ValueMin Nat4)

There is **only 1 situation you should use this.**

To make these the same type.

    [ atLeast1, in1To10 ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are: `Nat (ValueMin Nat1)`

    [ atLeast1
    , in1To10 |> InNat.toMin
    ]
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueMin min)

name: MinNat
comment:
2 situations where you use these operations instead of the ones in [`InNat`](InNat):

1.  Your value is of type `Nat (ValueMin ...)`

        nat3 |> MinNat.mul nat3
        --> Nat (ValueMin Nat3)

2.  The maximum isn't fixed

        divideBy : Nat (In (Nat1Plus minMinus1) max maybeN) -> --...


### compare

@docs is, isAtLeast, isAtMost


### modify

@docs subN, add, sub, addN

Every other available operation:


## drop information

@docs lowerMin


## modify

@docs toPower, remainderBy, mul, div


## compare

@docs atLeast

values.name: add
values.comment:
Add a `Nat (ValueMin ...)`. The second argument is the minimum if the added `Nat (ValueMin ...)`.

    nat5
        |> NNat.toMin
        |> MinNat.add atLeast2 nat2
    --> is of type Nat (ValueMin Nat7)

If you have don't the minimum added value at hand, use [`InNat.addLossy`](InNat#addLossy).

        |> InNat.atLeast nat10

values.name: div
values.comment:
Divide (`//`) by a `Nat (In ...)`.

  - `/ 0` is impossible

  - `x / d` is at most x

```
atMost7
    |> InNat.div nat3
--> Nat 2 of type Nat (ValueIn Nat0 (Nat7Plus a))
```
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus divMinMinus1) divMax divMaybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn TypeNats.Nat0 max)

values.name: isAtLeast
values.comment:
Is the `Nat (ValueMin ...)`

  - `equalOrGreater` than a `Nat` or

  - `less`?

```
factorial : MinNat min -> MinNat Nat1
factorial =
    MinNat.lowerMin nat0
        >> MinNat.isAtLeast nat1
            { min = nat0 }
            { less = \_ -> nat1 |> InNat.toMin
            , equalOrGreater =
                \atLeast1 ->
                    atLeast1
                        |> MinNat.mul
                            (factorial
                                (atLeast1 |> MinNat.subN nat1)
                            )
            }
```
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In triedMin (TypeNats.Nat1Plus triedMinMinus1PlusA) maybeN) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min (Nat.Bound.Is minToTriedMin Nat.Bound.To) triedMin x) } -> { less : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn min triedMinMinus1PlusA) -> result, equalOrGreater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueMin triedMin) -> result } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> result

values.name: mul
values.comment:
Multiply by a `Nat (In ...)` >= 1.
we know that if `a >= 1`, `x * a >= x`.

    atLeast5  |> MinNat.mul nat2
    --> Nat 10 of type Nat (ValueMin Nat5)

    atLeast2 |> MinNat.mul nat5
    --> Nat 10 of type Nat (ValueMin Nat2)

The maximum value of both factors can be `Infinity`.

values.name: remainderBy
values.comment:
The remainder after division.

  - `% 0` is impossible
  - `x % d` is at most `x`

```
atMost7 |> InNat.remainderBy nat3
--> Nat 1 of type Nat (In Nat0 (Nat7Plus a))
```

values.comment:
Subtract a `Nat (In ...)`. The second argument is the maximum of the subtracted `Nat (In ...)`.

    atLeast6 |> MinNat.sub in0To5 nat5
    --> is of type Nat (In Nat1 (Nat6Plus a))

If you have don't the maximum subtracted value at hand, use [`subLossy`](InNat#subLossy).

values.name: toPower
values.comment:
The `Nat (ValueMin ...) ^ a Nat (ValueMin ...)`.
We know that if `a >= 1  →  x ^ a >= x`

    atLeast5 |> MinNat.toPower nat2
    --> Nat 25 of type Nat (ValueMin Nat5)

    atLeast2 |> MinNat.toPower nat5
    --> Nat 25 of type Nat (ValueMin Nat2)

name: NNat

values.name: add
values.comment:
The `Nat (N ...)` plus another `Nat (N ...)`. Give the added value twice as a tuple.

    nat6 |> NNat.add ( nat5, nat5 )
    --> Nat 11 of type
    --> Nat
    -->     (N Nat11
    -->         (Is a To Nat11Plus a)
    -->         (Is b To Nat11Plus b)
    -->     )

This is only rarely useful, as you shouldn't

    inRangeXAndXPlus10 x =
        -- won't work
        isInRange x (x |> NNat.add ( nat10, nat10 ))

    isAtLeast10GreaterThan x =
        -- the only use case
        isAtLeast (x |> NNat.add ( nat10, nat10 ))

(examples don't compile, just for demonstration)
values.type: ( Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N addedN (Nat.Bound.Is n Nat.Bound.To) sumN (Nat.Bound.And atLeastN Nat.Bound.To atLeastSumN)), Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N addedN (Nat.Bound.Is aPlusN Nat.Bound.To) aPlusSum (Nat.Bound.And bPlusN Nat.Bound.To bPlusSum)) ) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N n (Nat.Bound.Is a Nat.Bound.To) aPlusN (Nat.Bound.And b Nat.Bound.To bPlusN)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N sumN (Nat.Bound.Is a Nat.Bound.To) aPlusSum (Nat.Bound.And b Nat.Bound.To bPlusSum))

values.name: sub
values.comment:
The `Nat (N ...)` plus another `Nat (N ...)`. Give the added value twice as a tuple.

    nat6 |> NNat.sub ( nat5, nat5 )
    --> Nat 1 of type
    --> Nat
    -->     (N Nat1
    -->         (Is a To Nat1Plus a)
    -->         (Is b To Nat1Plus b)
    -->     )

This is only rarely useful, as you shouldn't

    inRangeXMinus10ToX x =
        -- won't work
        isInRange (x |> NNat.sub ( nat10, nat10 )) x

    isAtLeast10LessThan x =
        -- the only use case
        isAtLeast (x |> NNat.sub ( nat10, nat10 ))

(examples don't compile, just for demonstration)
values.type: ( Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N subN (Nat.Bound.Is differenceN Nat.Bound.To) n (Nat.Bound.And atLeastDifferenceN Nat.Bound.To atLeastN)), Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N subN (Nat.Bound.Is aPlusDifferenceN Nat.Bound.To) aPlusN (Nat.Bound.And bPlusDifferenceN Nat.Bound.To bPlusN)) ) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N n (Nat.Bound.Is a Nat.Bound.To) aPlusN (Nat.Bound.And b Nat.Bound.To bPlusN)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N differenceN (Nat.Bound.Is a Nat.Bound.To) aPlusDifferenceN (Nat.Bound.And b Nat.Bound.To bPlusDifferenceN))

name: Nat

aliases.name: Nat
aliases.comment:
A **bounded** natural number (`>= 0`).


## value / return types

    -- >= 4
    Nat (ValueMin Nat4)

    -- 2 <= nat <= 12
    Nat (ValueIn Nat2 Nat12)

    -- = 3, & 3, described as a difference
    Nat (N Nat3 (Nat3Plus more) (Is a To (Nat3Plus a)) (Is b To (Nat3Plus b)))


## function argument types

    -- >= 4
    Nat (In (Nat4Plus minMinus4) max maybeN)

    -- 4 <= nat <= 5
    Nat (In (Nat4Plus minMinus4) Nat15 maybeN)

    -- any, just >= 0
    Nat range

    -- Not constuctable
    Nat Banana

values.name: abs
values.comment:
The absolute value of an `Int`, which is at least `Nat0`.

    MinNat.abs 16 --> Nat 16

    MinNat.abs -4 --> Nat 4

Really only use this if you want the absolute value.

    badLength list =
        List.length >> MinNat.abs

    goodLength =
        List.foldl
            (\_ ->
                MinNat.addN nat1
                    >> InNat.lowerMin nat0
            )
            (nat0 |> NNat.toMin)

If something like this isn't possible, use [`MinNat.intAtLeast`](MinNat#intAtLeast)!
values.type: Basics.Int -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueMin TypeNats.Nat0)

values.name: bi
values.comment:
Use the `Int` values of two `Nat`s to return a result.

    Nat.bi (>=) nat5 nat4 --> True

    Nat.bi (>=) nat5 nat40 --> False

Note, that you must give the `Nat`s in the same order you would give `Int`s.

Don't overuse this.
values.type: (Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> result) -> Nat.Nat aRange -> Nat.Nat bRange -> result

values.name: intAtLeast
values.comment:
A `Nat (Min ...)` from an `Int`; if the `Int < minimum`, `minimum` is returned.

    9 |> Nat.intAtLeast (nat3 |> NNat.toMin)
    --> Nat 9

    0 |> Nat.intAtLeast (nat3 |> NNat.toMin)
    --> Nat 3

You can also use this if you know an `Int` must be at least `minimum`.

But avoid it if you can do better, like

    goodLength =
        List.foldl
            (\_ ->
                MinNat.addN nat1
                    >> InNat.lowerMin nat0
            )
            (nat0 |> InNat.toMin)

If you want to handle the case `< minimum` yourself, use [`Nat.isIntAtLeast`](Nat#isIntAtLeast).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Basics.Int -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueMin min)

values.name: intInRange
values.comment:
A `Nat (In ...)` from an `Int`, **clamped** between a minimum & maximum.

  - if the `Int < minimum`, `minimum` is returned
  - if the `Int > maximum`, `maximum` is returned

```
clampBetween3And12 =
    Nat.intInRange nat3 nat12

9 |> clampBetween3And12 --> Nat 9

0 |> clampBetween3And12 --> Nat 3

99 |> clampBetween3And12 --> Nat 12
```

If you want to handle the cases `< minimum` & `> maximum` explicitly, use [`isIntInRange`](InNat#isIntInRange).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min firstMax maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In firstMax max upperMaybeN) -> Basics.Int -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn min max)

values.name: isIntAtLeast
values.comment:
If the `Int >= a minimum`, `Just` the `Nat (Min minimum)`, else `Nothing`.

    4 |> Nat.isIntAtLeast nat5 --> Nothing

    1234 |> Nat.isIntAtLeast nat5 --> Just (Nat 1234)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Basics.Int -> Maybe.Maybe (Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueMin min))

values.name: isIntInRange
values.comment:
An `Int` compared to a range from `first` to `last`.

    rejectOrAcceptUserInt =
        isIntInRange nat1 nat100
            { less = Err "must be >= 1"
            , greater = \_-> Err "must be <= 100"
            , inRange = Ok
            }

    rejectOrAcceptUserInt (NNat.toIn nat0)
    --> Err "must be >= 1"
values.type: { first : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In minFirst last firstMaybeN), last : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In last lastPlusA lastMaybeN) } -> { less : () -> result, greater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueMin (TypeNats.Nat1Plus last)) -> result, inRange : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn minFirst lastPlusA) -> result } -> Basics.Int -> result

values.name: random
values.comment: Generate a random `Nat (In ...)` in a range.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In firstMin lastMin firstMaybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In lastMin lastMax lastMaybeN) -> Random.Generator (Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn firstMin lastMax))

values.name: range
values.comment:
`Nat (In ...)`s from a first to a last value.

    from3To10 : List (Nat (ValueIn Nat3 (Nat10Plus a)))
    from3To10 =
        InNat.range nat3 nat10

    from3ToAtLeast10 : List (Nat (ValueMin Nat3))
    from3ToAtLeast10 =
        InNat.range nat3 atLeast10

The resulting `List` is never empty.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In firstMin lastMin firstMaybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In lastMin lastMax lastMaybeN) -> List.List (Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn firstMin lastMax))

values.name: theGreater
values.comment:
The greater of 2 `Nat`s. Works just like `Basics.max`.

    Nat.theGreater atLeast3
        (atLeast4 |> InNat.lowerMin nat3)
    --> Nat 4
values.type: Nat.Nat range -> Nat.Nat range -> Nat.Nat range

values.name: theSmaller
values.comment:
The smaller of 2 `Nat`s. Works just like `Basics.min`.

    Nat.theSmaller atLeast3
        (atLeast4 |> InNat.lowerMin nat3)
    --> Nat 3
values.type: Nat.Nat range -> Nat.Nat range -> Nat.Nat range

values.name: toInt
values.comment:
Convert a `Nat` to an `Int`.

    nat4 |> Nat.toInt --> 4
    nat4 |> NNat.toIn |> Nat.toInt --> 4
    nat4 |> NNat.toMin |> Nat.toInt --> 4

    compare : Nat range -> Nat range -> Order
    compare a b =
        compare (Nat.toInt a) (Nat.toInt b)
values.type: Nat.Nat range -> Basics.Int

name: Nat.Bound

aliases.name: And
aliases.comment:
`And a To b`: an exact value as the diffference `b - a`.

    N Nat5
        (Is myAge To)
        sistersAge
        (And mothersAge To fathersAge)

  - `myAge + 5 = sistersAge`
  - `mothersAge + 5 = fathersAge`

aliases.name: In
aliases.comment:
`In minimum maximum maybeN`: Somewhere within a minimum & maximum.

       ↓ minimum   ↓ maximum
    ⨯ [✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓] ⨯ ⨯ ⨯...

Note: maximum >= minimum for every existing `Nat (In min max ...)`:

    percent : Nat (In min Nat100 maybeN) -> Percent

→ `min <= Nat100`

If you want a number where you just care about the minimum, leave the `max` as a type _variable_.

       ↓ minimum    ↓ maximum or  →
    ⨯ [✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓...

Any natural number:

    Nat (In min max maybeN)

A number, at least 5:

    Nat (In (Nat5Plus minMinus5) max maybeN)

  - `max` could be a maximum value if there is one

  - `maybeN` could contain extra information for `N ...` if the number was exact

aliases.name: Is
aliases.comment:
An exact value as a diffference.

    N Nat5
        (Is myAge To)
        sistersAge
        (And mothersAge To fathersAge)

  - `myAge + 5 = sistersAge`
  - `mothersAge + 5 = fathersAge`

aliases.name: N
aliases.comment:
The most detailed description of a number at compile-time.

Every `N n atLeastN ...` is of type `In n atLeastN`.

Looking at the types

    nat0 :
        Nat
            (N
                -- 0 is the exact value at compile time
                Nat0
                -- 0 as a difference is a - a & b - b
                (Is a To)
                a
                (And b To b)
            )

An example is [`InNat.addN`](InNat#addN)

    addN :
        Nat (N added (Is min To) sumMin (And max To sumMax))
        -> Nat (In min max)
        -> Nat (In sumMin sumMax)

You can just ignore the second difference if you don't need it ([`MinNat.addN`](MinNat#addN)).

    addN :
        Nat (N added (Is min To) sumMin x)
        -> Nat (Min min)
        -> Nat (Min sumMin)

aliases.name: Only
aliases.comment:
Just the exact number.

Only useful as a function **argument** type.

Every `In NatXYZ (NatXYZPlus a) maybeN` is a `Only NatXYZ maybeN`.

    byte : Arr (Only maybeN Nat8) Bit -> Byte

→ A given [`Arr`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/indique/elm-bounded-array/latest/) must have _exact 8_ `Bit`s.

`Only` is useful for [`Arr`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/indique/elm-bounded-array/latest/)s,
but you will never need it in combination with `Nat`s.

aliases.name: To
aliases.comment:
No special meaning.

    Is a To b

→ distance `b - a`.

aliases.name: ValueIn
aliases.comment:
`ValueIn minimum maximum`: A value somewhere within a minimum & maximum. We don't know the exact value, though.

       ↓ minimum   ↓ maximum
    ⨯ [✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓] ⨯ ⨯ ⨯...

To be used as a return type, **not** as a function argument.

A number between 3 and 5

    Nat (ValueIn Nat3 (Nat5Plus a))

A number, at least 5:

    Nat (ValueIn Nat5 (Nat5Plus a))

aliases.name: ValueMin
aliases.comment:
Only **value / return types should be `Min`**.

Sometimes, you simply cannot compute a maximum.

    abs : Int -> Nat (ValueIn Nat0 ??)

This is where to use `Min`.

    abs : Int -> Nat (Min Nat0)

Every `Min min` is of type `In min ...`.

aliases.name: ValueOnly
aliases.comment:
Just the exact number.

Only useful as a **value & return** type.

    repeatOnly :
        Nat (Only n maybeN)
        -> element
        -> Arr (ValueOnly n) element

→ A given [`Arr`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/indique/elm-bounded-array/latest/) must has _exactly `n`_ `element`s.

`Only` is useful for [`Arr`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/indique/elm-bounded-array/latest/)s,
but you will never need it in combination with `Nat`s.
